target.title: #92589: fix(internal-runtime-context): wrap prompt-preface runtime context body in delimiters
target.body:
## Summary

Wrap the runtime context body in `buildRuntimeContextMessageContent` with `INTERNAL_RUNTIME_CONTEXT_BEGIN` / `END` delimiters so `stripInternalRuntimeContext` can fully remove leaked runtime context from user-visible surfaces.

## Root Cause

`buildRuntimeContextMessageContent` (in `runtime-context-prompt.ts`) constructs hidden runtime context prompt blocks using the "prompt preface" format:

```
OpenClaw runtime context for the immediately preceding user message.
This context is runtime-generated, not user-authored. Keep internal details private.

<runtime context body — relevant-memories, sender metadata, etc.>
```

When the model echoes this context back in its reply, `stripInternalRuntimeContext` strips the header and notice lines via `stripRuntimeContextPromptPreface`, but the **body** (containing sensitive data like `<relevant-memories>`, `Sender (untrusted metadata)`, `Conversation info`) was not wrapped in the delimiters that `stripDelimitedBlock` handles. The body leaked through into channel card rendering (Feishu streaming cards).

**Fix**: Wrap the runtime context body in `<<<BEGIN_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>` / `<<<END_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>` delimiters. `stripInternalRuntimeContext` already calls `stripDelimitedBlock` first (which handles nested delimiters correctly), then `stripLegacyInternalRuntimeContext`, then `stripRuntimeContextPromptPreface`. With the body now delimited, the entire context block is removed regardless of escape path.

## Real behavior proof

### behavior

When a runtime context block (containing `<relevant-memories>`, `Sender (untrusted metadata)`, `Conversation info`, system timestamps) is echoed in a reply, `stripInternalRuntimeContext` now completely removes the entire block instead of leaving the context body visible.

### environment

- OS: Linux x64
- Runtime: Node.js v24.13.1
- Test runner: vitest v4.1.7
- openclaw: fix/issue-92589-* branch on top of origin/main

### steps

1. Build runtime context with sensitive metadata via `buildRuntimeContextSystemContext`
2. Simulate model echoing it in a reply
3. Run `stripInternalRuntimeContext` on the leaked reply
4. Verify all sensitive data is removed, actual reply text preserved

### observedResult

**Reproduction script output:**
```
========================================================================
Real behavior proof — #92589
========================================================================

Built runtime context (delimiters now wrap the body):
---
OpenClaw runtime context for the immediately preceding user message.
This context is runtime-generated, not user-authored. Keep internal details private.

<<<BEGIN_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>
Sender (untrusted metadata):
{"open_id": "ou_abc123", "name": "Alice"}
Conversation info (untrusted metadata):
{"chat_id": "oc_xyz789", "message_id": "om_456"}
<relevant-memories>
Memory: Bob uses Python for backend work.
</relevant-memories>
<<<END_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>
---

After stripInternalRuntimeContext:
---
Here is my analysis of the data:

Would you like me to proceed further?
---

Contains <relevant-memories>:     ✅ NO (clean)
Contains Sender metadata:        ✅ NO (clean)
Contains Conversation metadata:  ✅ NO (clean)
Contains user open_id:           ✅ NO (clean)
Contains context header:         ✅ NO (clean)
Actual reply text preserved:              ✅ YES
```

**Test suite:**
```
runtime-context-prompt.test.ts:  21/21 passed
replay-history.test.ts:          all passed
sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts:  142/142 passed
internal-runtime-context.test.ts: 5/5 passed
```

**Production change:**
```typescript
// runtime-context-prompt.ts — buildRuntimeContextMessageContent
return [
  params.kind === "runtime-event"
    ? OPENCLAW_RUNTIME_EVENT_HEADER
    : OPENCLAW_NEXT_TURN_RUNTIME_CONTEXT_HEADER,
  OPENCLAW_RUNTIME_CONTEXT_NOTICE,
  "",
  INTERNAL_RUNTIME_CONTEXT_BEGIN,   // ← NEW
  params.runtimeContext,
  INTERNAL_RUNTIME_CONTEXT_END,     // ← NEW
].join("\n");
```

## Regression Test Plan

- [x] `runtime-context-prompt.test.ts` — 21/21
- [x] `replay-history.test.ts` — passed
- [x] `sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts` — 142/142
- [x] `internal-runtime-context.test.ts` — 5/5
- [x] `stripInternalRuntimeContext` correctly removes delimited blocks (existing behavior, unchanged)
- [x] Change is minimal (3 lines added to production code)

---

*AI-assisted: built with Claude Code*
target.author: zhangqueping
